title: Cannot play kube files (k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5 fails)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind bug

**Description**

I tried to play a yaml file which used to work (up until Fedora 33). After migrating to Fedora 34 (which comes with podman 3.1.2 and crun 0.19.1 and conmon 2.0.27) this stopped working. I was able to reproduce the problem with a minimal yaml file.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
test.yaml:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod

metadata:
  name: test

spec:
  containers:
  - name: redis
    image: arm32v7/redis:alpine
```
1. podman play kube test.yaml --log-level=debug --runtime=crun &> [test.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/6489397/test.log)
2. podman ps -a &> [ps.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/6489396/ps.log)

**Describe the results you received:**
```
error starting container 75bdc81db921fc8e95f69e5a21648c4f7cb9613cd9c014d14933338751d8a2a4: `/usr/bin/crun start 75bdc81db921fc8e95f69e5a21648c4f7cb9613cd9c014d14933338751d8a2a4` failed: exit status 1
```
This is the container with the image `k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5`.

**Describe the results you expected:**
The startup of the container should be successful.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Currently I use cgroups V2.
Previously, with systemd.unified_cgroup_hierarchy=0 in the kernel arguments, the behaviour and problem was the same.
I already tried `setsebool -P container_manage_cgroup true`.
I tried running the podman play command from an own systemd service as well as from command line as root user.
Running simple podman containers works, e.g.
```
	mkdir ~/mycontent
	chcon -t container_var_lib_t ~/mycontent
	podman run -v ~/mycontent:/content fedora touch /content/file`
```
runs without error and creates an empty file `~/mycontent/file`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.16.3
Built:        Wed May 12 21:29:45 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/arm
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

      crun version 0.19.1

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.1.2-3.fc34.armv7hl
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
podman is running on a Hardkernel Odroid HC1.

comments.body:
`container state improper` on attempting to start the infra container. That one's new to me.

Tried locally, doesn't reproduce, so this is almost certainly environment related somehow. You mention systemd - are you running `podman play kube` within a unit file? Are you running as root, or rootless?

Hi @mheon,
I mostly tested from command line as root user, but originally encountered the problem when running from systemd unit (see additional information above).
Best regards,
Stephan

I was able to reproduce this problem on a raspberry pi 3 running Fedora 34 ARM (the configuration is mostly automated and thus very similar). So either it is due to some deliberate configuration choices or related to Fedora ARM distribution.
On my Fedora 34 x64_64, `podman play kube test.yaml` runs smoothly without error. So +1 for the ARM theory.
@baude Since you're knee-deep in aarch64 land, would you care to take a look?

I just retried with podman-3.2.0-5.fc34.armv7hl.
Unfortunately the problem still is the same.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@stephanritscher Is this still an issue?
@baude did you ever get a chance to look at this?

@rhatdan Yes, I'm still interested and unfortunately I can confirm the bug with podman-3.2.3-2.fc34.armv7hl.
@baude I just setup an aarch64 vm with (as far as I can tell) the same settings and podman-3.2.3-2.fc34.aarch64. In this system, I was able to start up a pod using the same test.yaml successfully.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99 weren't you looking into dropping the pause image and just shipping the execuable with Podman and creating the infra container on the fly?
I think @vrothberg wants to work on that.
As long as someone does, I would like to remove the need to worry about particular images if possible.
There's a Jira card for it.  I will start as soon as it's moved to the sprint.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@vrothberg Could you link the pause stuff you are working on to fix this Issue?

Sorry, I forgot to confirm that the patch works.

Thanks a lot!
